description:
## What

Eliminate the dead `iconst.i64 0` emitted by every native fn's entry block. Today `src/ir_codegen.rs:2550` materializes a placeholder so the rest of `compile_fn`'s body — which has ~9 references to `frame_ptr` — can use a non-Option `ir::Value`. Native fns never read it; Cranelift's DCE removes it post-optimization; the pre-opt CLIF dump still shows it.

## Design (tighter; enforced invariant)

Change `frame_ptr` in `compile_fn` to `Option<ir::Value>`:
- `None` in the native branch.
- `Some(block_params[0])` in the uniform branch.

Change the emit-helpers (`emit_return`, `emit_call`, `emit_tail_call`, `emit_receive`) to take `Option<ir::Value>` and `.expect("emit_X reached from native-fn body — natively_callable invariant violated")` internally. Inline reads in `compile_fn` (Term::CallClosure's `load v0+16`, Term::Receive's `load v0+16`, the uniform-cont-write-back's `load v0+16` at ~2865) also `.expect()` at point of use.

This is strictly tighter than the previous "silent placeholder" shape: any code path that *would* try to read frame_ptr in a native fn — today indicating a bug — surfaces immediately as a panic at codegen time, instead of silently emitting a load-from-zero.

## Why it's safe (researched)

The `natively_callable` invariant (parking.rs + heap-safe fixed point at ir_codegen.rs:1490-1560) already guarantees that a native fn body cannot contain:
- `Term::Return` going through `emit_return` (handled by `if is_native { return_ } else { emit_return }`).
- `Term::Call` or `Term::TailCall` with a uniform callee or uniform cont (`body_ok` rejects).
- `Term::CallClosure`, `Term::TailCallClosure`, `Term::Receive` (`_ => false` in `body_ok`).

I audited each of the 9 `frame_ptr` consumer sites in `compile_fn`'s terminator-lowering match. Every one is reached only when the surrounding context is uniform-caller (either `else` of `if is_native` or a terminator excluded from native fns by parking). Documented in the audit table at the end of this description.

## Tests (TDD)

1. **CLIF assertion** (extend `add1_has_no_int_box_or_unbox_anywhere` or add new): `add1_s2` and `k_2_s3`'s CLIF must NOT contain `iconst.i64 0` (since neither uses any constant 0 in its body). Asserts the dead placeholder is gone.
2. **Behavioural**: `cargo run -- run fixtures/add1.fz` still prints 42.
3. **Spec parity + fixture matrix**: no regressions.

## Acceptance

- `cargo test` green, no warnings.
- `add1.fz` CLIF: native fns (`add1_s2`, `k_2_s3`) have no `iconst.i64 0` in their entry.
- No `frame_ptr` panics in any test.

## Audit (9 sites in compile_fn referencing frame_ptr)

| Line | Site | Reachable in native? | Why |
|------|------|----------------------|-----|
| 2565/2570/2574 | entry-param loads | No (these lines live inside the *uniform* branch of the `if is_native` setup; not reached for native fns) | Structural |
| 2759 | `emit_return` (Term::Return) | No | Inside `else` of `if is_native` |
| 2848 | `emit_return` (Call native-chain inner uniform) | No | Inside `else` of `if is_native` |
| 2865 | inline `load v0+16` (Call native-callee uniform-cont) | No | natively_callable requires cont native if caller native |
| 2902 | `emit_call` (Call uniform-callee) | No | natively_callable requires callee native if caller native |
| 2946 | `emit_return` (TailCall native-callee inner uniform) | No | Inside `else` of `if is_native` |
| 2990 | `emit_tail_call` (TailCall uniform-callee) | No | natively_callable requires callee native if caller native |
| 3012 | inline `load v0+16` (Term::CallClosure) | No | CallClosure excluded by `body_ok` |
| 3059 | inline `load v0+16` (Term::Receive) | No | Receive excluded by `body_ok` |
| 3098 | `emit_receive` (Term::Receive) | No | same |

## Out of scope

Making `main` native (eliminates main's trampoline-return plumbing) — separate ticket if/when motivated.
title: Native fns: enforce no-frame_ptr invariant; remove dead iconst placeholder
